sections.paragraph:
ἐργασία, ας, ἡ (s. prec. entry; Pind.+; ins, pap, LXX, En 8:1; TestSol, Joseph., Just.).

① engagement in some activity or behavior with sustained interest, practice, pursuit τινός of someth. (Pla., Gorg., 450c τ. τεχνῶν; Ps.-Pla., Eryx., 404b; Sir 6:19; 38:34) εἰς ἐ. ἀκαθαρσίας πάσης for the practice of all kinds of sinful things Eph 4:19. πολλαπλασιάζειν τὴν ἐ. do many kinds of work GHb 297, 21 (s. also 4 below).

② manner of activity, working, function (Pla., Prot., 353d τῆς ἡδονῆς) τῶν ἀγγέλων Hm 6, 2, 6 cj. Gebhardt-Harnack-Zahn on basis of Lat. and Ethiopic mss. (Whittaker: ἐνέργεια).

③ business activity, trade, business (X., Oec. 6, 8; Diod S 1, 80, 1; PLond III, 906, 6 p. 108 [128 a.d.]; PFay 93, 7; Sir 7:15) Ac 19:25 (Arrian, Peripl. 21, 1 ξόανον τῆς παλαιᾶς ἐργασίας).

④ proceeds of work or activity profit, gain (X., Mem. 3, 10, 1; Polyb. 4, 50, 3; Artem. 4, 57 ἔχειν … ἐργασίαν=have profits or wages; PGM 4, 2438; Wsd 13:19; Jos., Bell. 2, 109) Ac 16:19. παρέχειν ἐργασίαν τινί bring profit to someone vs. 16; 19:24 (cp. the litotes Aesop, Fab. 112 H.=56 P. of a μάγος οὐ μικρὸν βίον πορίζουσα). πολλαπλ. τ. ἐ. multiply the gains GHb 297, 21 (s. 1 above).

⑤ For δὸς ἐργασίαν Lk 12:58 s. δίδωμι 17a (ἐ.=pains: Jos., Ant. 3, 35 μὴ σὺν πόνῳ μηδʼ ἐργασίᾳ).—DELG s.v. ἔργον. M-M.

prec. prec. = preceding

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

GHb GHb = Gospel according to the Hebrews—List 1

Hm Hm = Mandates

  1. cj. = conjecture(d by)

Zahn Zahn = TZ., various works—List 6

Lat. Lat. = Latin

Whittaker Whittaker = MW.; s. H—List 1

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Diod Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

PFay PFay = Fayûm Towns and Their Papyri—List 4

Arrian Arrian = Flavius Arrianus, I–II a.d.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Aesop Aesop = a calque for a variety of lit. associated with the name of Aesop—List 5

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 390
